text.title: Problem Validation Process
text.summary: Evidence types ranked by signal strength, five validation criteria with thresholds, and the 5-step Proceed/Pivot/Kill process.

rule.statement: Rank evidence by source: Verbatim quotes from interviews (strong), Forum discussions (medium), Review complaints (medium), Search volume (medium), Competitor existence (weak). Use at least 3 independent source types; single-source validation is fragile.
rule.rationale: Each source type has a different bias; forum signals skew toward vocal minorities; search volume signals lag actual demand; competitor existence confirms someone thinks the problem is real but not that it remains unsolved.

rule.statement: A problem passes validation when ALL five criteria exceed threshold: Frequency (weekly or more), Intensity (7+ out of 10), Willingness to Pay (yes, from at least 4 out of 5 interviewees), Search Behavior (search volume exists), Competition (at least one competitor exists).
rule.rationale: A problem that is frequent but low-intensity is a minor annoyance; a problem that is intense but rare is an edge case. Both frequency AND intensity must exceed threshold independently before proceeding.

rule.statement: Run: (1) state problem hypothesis; (2) define kill threshold before collecting evidence; (3) collect 10+ data points across 3+ source types; (4) assess each piece of evidence against the five criteria; (5) decide PROCEED, PIVOT (with direction), or KILL.
rule.rationale: Defining the kill threshold before collection prevents confirmation bias from distorting the decision after evidence is gathered.

example.note: Hypothesis: Freelancers struggle tracking time across projects. Evidence: 8/10 interviews confirmed, avg 5 hrs/week lost, 4/5 would pay. All five criteria exceeded threshold. Decision: PROCEED to solution validation.

text.reference: LLM prompt to score evidence and output filled report
